summary: Microsoft privacy guidance documents the Start_TrackProgs registry value under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced and states that setting it to 0 turns off app launch tracking for Start and Search. The current app writes that exact documented value.

setting.name: App Launch Tracking
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can track which apps you launch so Start and Search can personalize some results. People turn it off when they want less app-usage tracking.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it to reduce usage tracking and Start-menu personalization based on what they open.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off can reduce Start and Search personalization based on app launches, but some users accept that in exchange for less tracking.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows privacy guidance documents Start_TrackProgs under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced and states that setting it to 0 turns off the "Let Windows track app launches to improve Start and search results" toggle. The app writes that same documented value.

allowed_values.label: Windows managed UI state
allowed_values.meaning: The preference is not explicitly overridden in the registry and stays under the Windows UI toggle.

allowed_values.label: App launch tracking disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Turns off "Let Windows track app launches to improve Start and search results."

targets.notes: Microsoft documents Start_TrackProgs = 0 as the registry control for turning off app launch tracking.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes Start_TrackProgs = 0 under Explorer\Advanced, matching the Microsoft-documented toggle-off value.

windows_defaults.label: Windows managed default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows user profiles using the normal Privacy settings UI

states.rationale: Leave the preference unset and let the Settings UI manage the tracking toggle.

windows_defaults.notes: The registry value is only needed when forcing the privacy toggle off.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
avoid_for: Users who want to disable app launch tracking

states.rationale: Leave the toggle under normal Windows control.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to manage the app launch tracking setting via the UI.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want app launch tracking disabled.
tradeoffs: Start and Search can still use app-launch history for personalization.
No explicit registry override is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable app launch tracking
intended_for: Users who do not want Start/Search personalization based on app usage
avoid_for: Users who want the most personalized Start and Search experience

states.rationale: Use the Microsoft-documented registry value to turn the tracking off.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to stop Windows from tracking app launches for Start/Search personalization.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You rely on app-launch-based personalization in Start or Search.
tradeoffs: Windows no longer tracks app launches for this feature.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Manage connections from Windows operating system components to Microsoft services
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents Start_TrackProgs under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced and states that setting it to 0 turns off app launch tracking for Start and Search.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: Start_TrackProgs registry preference item
evidence.summary: Microsoft Open Specifications document the Start_TrackProgs registry path under Explorer\Advanced.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes Start_TrackProgs = 0 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft's Windows privacy guidance documents the Start_TrackProgs registry value under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced and states that setting it to 0 turns off app launch tracking. The app writes that same documented value.

validation_proof.notes: The Microsoft privacy guidance lists the exact registry path and value to turn off "Let Windows track app launches to improve Start and search results."
